problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
hypothesis: Telomerase activation/inhibition in target cells may resolve Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? by blocking at the molecular level.

experiment.protocol: Human organoid model with 27 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Real knowledge is to know the extent of one's ignorance. — Confucius
